items.title: India has all ingredients to be a full-stack AI leader: Sam Altman
items.description: Sam Altman noted India’s rapid adoption of AI tools, saying the country now has ”100 million weekly active users”, the second-largest base after the US.

items.title: ixigo acquires majority stake in Spain's online travel solutions firm for Rs 125 crore
items.description: ixigo will acquire an upfront majority stake of 60% in Trenes, the second-largest train OTA in Spain, marking a significant milestone in ixigo’s international expansion into Europe.

items.title: Smartphone maker Nothing doubles down on India, CEO Carl Pei says company investing more
items.description: The company opened its first flagship offline store in Bengaluru and plans to expand its physical retail footprint further, signaling a deeper long-term commitment to India beyond its online-first beginnings.

items.title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei bets on AI-native OS by 2028 to challenge iOS-Android duopoly
items.description: Pei, who founded Nothing in October 2020 with Akis Evangelidis, argued that true disruption would require rethinking the operating system itself, not just embedding AI tools within current platforms.

items.title: Nothing opens first flagship store in Bengaluru, bets bigger on offline India push
items.description: The outlet also houses a content studio for creators, reinforcing Nothing’s community-led marketing strategy and its strong digital-native following.

items.title: Uday Kotak appointed Chairman of GIFT City by Gujarat government
items.description: Uday Kotak succeeds Dr Hasmukh Adhia, IAS (Retd.), who had been serving as Chairman (Non-Executive) of the Board of Directors of GIFT City since June 19, 2023

items.title: Morgan Stanley mulls $500 million India fund, shifts some assets
items.description: Morgan Stanley Investment Management invested in Omega in 2024 with a minority ownership and bought a controlling stake in RG Scientific that same year.

items.title: Nvidia shares go cold even as Big Tech spending on AI balloons
items.description: Wall Street analyst estimates for Nvidia’s revenue and earnings in 2026 have barely budged since the tech giants revealed their mega-spending plans

items.title: India fines Air India $110,350 in Airbus incident, says lapse eroded public confidence
items.description: An Airbus A320 flew passengers between New Delhi, Bengaluru, Mumbai and Hyderabad on November 24 to 25 without the mandatory Airworthiness Review Certificate, or ARC, a key permit issued annually by the regulator after a plane passes safety and compliance checks.

items.title: GST reset powers entry-level bike revival; Hero MotoCorp hits 8-yr high market share
items.description: A key driver has been the surge in first-time buyers. Hero said the share of first-time customers has risen from around 75-80%, attributing the shift to GST rationalisation that effectively rolled back two-wheeler prices to levels seen three years ago

items.title: InsuranceDekho owner is said to plan $250 million India IPO
items.description: The Gurugram-based insurance aggregator has held recent discussions with investment banks about a potential listing and is expected to appoint advisers in the coming weeks

items.title: IBM to triple US entry-level hiring for roles seen as replaceable by AI
items.description: While the company declined to disclose specific hiring figures, it said the expansion will be 'across the board,' affecting a wide range of departments

items.title: Indian Hotels Company: Strategic bet on hospitality boom
items.description: The company is expanding aggressively and has a pipeline to add ~30,200 keys over the next few years

items.title: ZFCVL: Resilient Q3, cyclical recovery in sight
items.description: The management remains optimistic about outperforming industry growth, supported by a robust pipeline of new products, stronger aftermarket business, and favourable regulatory developments

items.title: Muthoot Finance Q3 FY26 — All that glitters is gold
items.description: The company looks set to surpass its full-year gold loan growth target for FY26

items.title: LG Electronics India Q3: Margins take a hit as post-festive demand stays soft
items.description: Long-term leadership intact, positioned for summer recovery and export acceleration

items.title: HUL Q3 FY26: Growth speaks volumes about the quarterly show
items.description: The company is betting on a business transformation and is guiding to a higher sales growth for FY27

items.title: ByteDance's new AI video model goes viral as China looks for second DeepSeek moment
items.description: ByteDance, which officially unveiled Seedance 2.0 on Thursday, said in a statement that the system was designed for professional film, e-commerce, and advertising productions

items.title: Memory price surge may slow telcos’ fiber, FWA rollout in 2026
items.description: FWA has emerged as a key growth engine for home broadband in India, particularly in regions where fibre deployment is slow or economically unviable

items.title: Spicejet slips into net loss in Q3 FY26
items.description: Consolidated revenue from operations grew by 14 percent for the reporting quarter to Rs 1,345.46 crore as against Rs 1,178.76 crore posted in the same quarter last year

items.title: Apollo Hospitals Q3FY26: Scaling up with profitability discipline
items.description: Capacity expansion, digital push power next leg of growth

items.title: Lenskart Q3 — Execution is clear, valuation still demanding
items.description: The third-quarter performance confirms that the company has entered a structurally stronger phase where growth and profitability are improving together

items.title: Titan Company: The market leader has a bright outlook
items.description: Given the sharp increase in gold prices, Titan is light-weighting as well as introducing low-carat jewellery to retain price points, thereby sustaining growth

items.title: Apollo Hospitals outperforms in Q3, focus turns to capacity expansion
items.description: The new hospitals are expected to incur sizeable loss in the first year of operations

items.title: Britannia Q3 FY26: Sustained growth recovery, stable margins on the cards
items.description: A diversified portfolio, strong execution make the outlook positive
